# Private markets: an ideal alternative

04.10.2021

Share this article:

## Le Temps (04.10.2021) - Conditions for private markets, which started to take off in the 1990s, are now better than they have ever been. A large proportion of the alternative investment universe – all non-traditional asset classes – is covered by private markets. [...] The programme is aimed at all the Bank’s staff, regardless of their role. It’s made up of eight 45-minute training modules that are available online in both English and French. It’s an innovative, interactive format accompanied by videos and questionnaires. The training course covers definitions, norms, current and future regulations, what steps UBP is taking and its investor offering. That may sound like a lot, but, overall, it only adds up to a single day of training, it’s just that everyone can take part at their own pace and fit it in with their work schedule. 

### Is this programme compulsory?

results.title: Prioritizing Alternatives in AHP | lateralworks
results.content:
Because rank reversal has caused such debate, a new mode was introduced called the Ideal mode(it's correct name is Ideal synthesis mode). The Ideal mode preserves the ranking of the alternatives, while its distributive property allows the actual ranks (priorities) to change. This is done by making the winner/highest ranked alternative take the priority of the covering objective(e.g. if alternative #1 was the winner and the objective priority was 40%, it would receive a priority of 40%) while the remaining percentage (in our example 60% = 100%-40%) is distributed proportionally to their ranking. Thus, the ranking of the alternatives is preserved, while the priorities can change based on their rankings.

By default, decisionAccelerator operates in Ideal model.

## The Math [...] Step 3: Sum the weighted alternatives

The next step is simply, for each alternative, sum the values from Step 2 for each objective. For the IDeal and Grid mode, this is:

IPA1 = (O1 x A1) + (O2x A1) + ...

where IPA1 is the interim ranking (or priority) of Alternative 1. Note that we call it an interim priority because some additional normalization is required after in order to calculate the final priorities. For the Distributed mode, this is

IPA1 = (O1 x A1) / (ΣAn-o1 + 1) + (O2 x A1) / (ΣAn-o2 + 1) + ...

Step 4: Calculate final alternative priorities

For the Grid mode, the resulting IPAnis the final priority. This in itself is somewhat meaningless (because the priorities don't add up to 100%), so they need to be further normalized. [...] In Distributed mode, the approach is different because of the way the weights are distributed amongst the alternatives. We perform the same normalization calculation as above (Step 1 & 2), but the result is then divided by the sum of the normalized rankings + 1 (the '1' being introduced as the ideal alternative), e.g (O1 x A1) / (ΣAn-o1 + 1), where ΣAn-o1 is the sum of the normalized ranked values for Objective 1.

Table 2: Normalizing the rankings and prioritizing the alternatives for Objective 1 in Distributive mode

Table 2: Normalizing the rankings and prioritizing the alternatives for Objective 1 in Distributive mode

Step 3: Sum the weighted alternatives

results.title: Direct Cremation Is the Cost-Effective Alternative
results.content:
Every family should come to terms with what it can afford to spend on these kind of affairs. There is no shame in being honest about any number of limitations. Again, these things can get really expensive. And you might not be in a position to afford all the ins and outs associated with a funeral or memorial service. You may, for example, be focused on saving money for retirement. You may have other expenses that are extremely important. Sometimes it simply isn’t possible to spend a great deal of money during a difficult time like this. Don’t worry. Many families find themselves in very similar situations. That’s why direct cremation is such an ideal alternative for so many. Focusing on the cremation alone is a far more affordable and cost-effective strategy. [...] By Cremation Society of Milwaukee • September 23, 2024

Discover how to select the ideal cremation service provider. Learn key factors to consider for a compassionate and informed decision. Call us now for more info!

Share by: [...] Remember that there are always other ways to honor and remember a loved one. For example, you can personally plan a very small event for close friends or family at a time of your choosing. It doesn’t have to be a funeral. It doesn’t have to be a memorial service. And it doesn’t have to be anything fancy. You can simply make some very casual arrangements for those who were close to the deceased. It may be a great opportunity for you to share some memories and support one another. After all, that’s really what a funeral is all about. There is no need for a formal ceremony, especially when something like that appears to be out of reach financially.

results.title: What is Alternative Investing? — Flat Fee & Fee-Only Financial Advisor Bryan Minogue | Madison, WI
results.content:
Stagflationary environments are marked by slowing economic growth and high inflation. Fortunately, these environments have been rare, with the most sustained period of stagflation occurring during the 1970s. However, 2022 was a recent reminder of how inflationary shocks can result in stocks and bonds declining in tandem. Global stocks declined 18% in 2022 and investment-grade bonds declined 13%.

An ideal "alternative investment" for a stock and bond portfolio would:

1. Produce positive returns over time
2. Exhibit low correlation to stocks and bonds
3. Perform well in stagflationary environments

## What are the benefits of alternative investments?

Adding diverse, uncorrelated assets to a portfolio can maintain or enhance the return of the portfolio while reducing volatility or risk. [...] The term "alternative investing" is often used as a catch-all phrase for any assets outside of traditional stocks and bonds. While alternative investments can add significant value to traditional portfolios, the investments must meet specific criteria to be effective. In this post, I will walk through how I define alternatives, which asset classes meet these criteria, and which asset classes do not.

## What is Alternative Investing?

When I consider "alternatives," I look for asset classes that will complement and diversify core stock and bond holdings. Ideally, an alternative is an uncorrelated asset that performs well when stocks and bonds struggle.
